calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment And Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Mar-11-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANTHONY J. DIPIERO OF HUNT & HENRIQUES, TEL. NO. (408) 362-2282 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SEPIDEH VALENTINO Motion To Vacate Judgment And Set Aside Default is Denied. Judgment was entered in August 2014. Defendant did not move to vacate the judgment until January 2015. Defendant offers no explanation for the delay. Thus, Defendant has not moved to vacate the judgment within a reasonable time, as required by CCP sec. 473(b). (See, e.g., Benjamino v. Dalmo Mfg. Co. (1948) 31 Cal.2d 523, 528, holding that while the trial court has discretion in determining if the motion is made within a reasonable time period, "there must be some showing-some evidence-as the basis for the exercise of such discretion.") Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Department 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6840.

Anthony DiPiero, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff CITIBANK (SOUTHDAKOTA) N.A.
No appearance on behalf or by Defendant SEPIDEH VALENTINO.
Brian Billings sworn and examined on behalf of the Plaintiff.
After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of CITIBANK (SOUTHDAKOKTA) N.A. and against Defendant SEPIDEH VALENTINO as follows:
Damages $35,673.88
Pre-Judgment Interest $0.00
Attorney's Fees $0.00
Costs $492.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $36,165.88
Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SEPIDEH VALENTINO's Motion For Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment is Granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 473(d) and 473.5. Plaintiff has shown that she did not live at either 601 Van Ness Avenue or 765 Market Street and was never served with the summons and complaint or any other papers in this action. The Court signed the order that was emailed to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976.

No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued on the court's own motion to December 20, 2012 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of its opposition to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than November 30, 2012 at 4 pm, or the motion will be granted. The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not reported
